questions.question: Which type of joint is the glenohumeral joint?
questions.explanation: The shoulder (glenohumeral) joint is a multiaxial ball and socket synovial joint allowing wide range of movement.

questions.question: The anatomical neck of the humerus is the most common site of humeral fractures.
questions.explanation: False! Humeral fractures most frequently occur at the surgical neck, which is distal to the anatomical neck.

questions.question: Name the major bony landmark on the proximal ulna that forms the point of the elbow:
questions.explanation: The olecranon process of the ulna projects behind the humerus, serving as the insertion for the triceps tendon.

questions.question: Fracture of the humerus shaft is most likely to injure which nerve?
questions.explanation: The radial nerve runs in the spiral groove directly against the humeral shaft, making it highly vulnerable in shaft fractures.

questions.question: Ape hand deformity is caused by injury to the ulnar nerve.
questions.explanation: False! Ape hand deformity (loss of thumb opposition) is caused by median nerve injury.

questions.question: Arrange the carpal bones in order from lateral to medial in the proximal row:
questions.explanation: The proximal row of carpal bones from lateral (thumb side) to medial (pinky side) is: Scaphoid, Lunate, Triquetrum, Pisiform.

levels.title: Knee Instability Tests
levels.description: ACL, PCL, and Meniscus diagnostic tests

questions.question: What is the most sensitive clinical test for diagnosing an acute ACL tear?
questions.explanation: The Lachman test, done at 20-30 degrees of knee flexion, is widely considered the most sensitive clinical exam for ACL integrity.

questions.question: Match the special test with its target anatomy:
questions.explanation: Correct matches: Lachman tests ACL, McMurray tests Meniscus, Valgus tests MCL, Posterior Sag tests PCL.

questions.question: A positive Thompson test is indicative of an Achilles tendon rupture.
questions.explanation: True! Squeezing the calf normally causes plantarflexion. Lack of plantarflexion (positive test) signifies a rupture.

questions.question: Pain on passive internal rotation of an abducted and forward-flexed shoulder describes which test?
questions.explanation: Hawkins-Kennedy pushes the supraspinatus tendon against the coracoacromial ligament, triggering impingement pain.

questions.question: Which fracture is characterized by a fracture of the distal radius with dorsal displacement of the distal fragment?
questions.explanation: Colles' fracture shows classic 'dinner fork' deformity due to dorsal displacement. Smith's fracture has volar displacement.

questions.question: A Galeazzi fracture involves a fracture of the shaft of the radius with dislocation of the distal radioulnar joint (DRUJ).
questions.explanation: True! Remember GRIM: Galeazzi = Radius fracture + DRUJ dislocation. Monteggia = Ulna fracture + PRUJ dislocation.
